records.ety: [L. contumacia, fr. contumax, -acis, insolent; prob. akin to contemnere to despise: cf. F. contumace. Cf. Contemn.]

defs: Stubborn perverseness; pertinacious resistance to authority.
quotes: The bishop commanded him . . . to be thrust into the stocks for his manifest and manifold contumacy.

defs: A willful contempt of, and disobedience to, any lawful summons, or to the rules and orders of court, as a refusal to appear in court when legally summoned.
